review_metadata.summary: Post-bugfix re-review (Phase 5u Wave 1). Table 1 numerical values now RECONCILE with pipeline after the k_H^2 fix in compute_dissipative_correction. All new Lean theorems (GammaH, deltaDissFromTransport*, gammaH_*) exist in SecondOrderSK.lean with no active sorry. Main residual issues: (1) citation keys use Author:Year format (e.g. 'Steinhauer:2019') but CITATION_REGISTRY uses AuthorYear (e.g. 'Steinhauer2016'/'deNova2019') — 0 of 11 bibitems directly resolve against registry; (2) Zaremba-regime estimate (eq:gamma_estimates_expanded, eq:delta_diss_zaremba) is dimensionally suspect — gamma_1 quoted in [s^-1] but plugged into a formula that expects [m^2/s] in all other pipeline uses, yielding an apparent 10^-4..10^-3 only if gamma_1 is reinterpreted as a rate; (3) all 15 platform parameters remain human-unverified (blocks submission, not draft); (4) a __main__ demo block in transonic_background.py still passes gamma_beliaev [s^-1] as gamma_1 [m^2/s] — produces inflated delta_diss~1e3 in console output, not used for Table 1 but a foot-gun; (5) paper body's eq:hawking_damping and eq:delta_diss_explicit drop the k_H^2 factor that was the point of the bugfix, so body text is mildly inconsistent with the implementation.

layer_1_numerical_claims.recompute_method: uv run python against src.core.transonic_background.solve_transonic_background + compute_dissipative_correction with beliaev_transport_coefficients giving gamma_1, gamma_2 in [m^2/s]

table_1_verification.note: Rounding-level; paper value 4.8 is 1-decimal rounded from 4.7578
Paper value has only 1 sig fig (0.006); rounding from 0.0058

inline_numerical_claims.support: Steinhauer Bel=2.4e-5, Heidelberg Bel=1.6e-3 — range captures all three platforms

inline_numerical_claims.claim: spin-sonic enhancement (c_density/c_spin)^2 ~ 100 brings delta_diss to O(10^-2)
inline_numerical_claims.support: Arithmetic: 100 x (1e-5 .. 1e-3) = 1e-3 .. 1e-1; paper later writes 'O(10^-1)' in the kinetic regime. No pipeline computation uses actual spin-channel transport coefficients — it's a scaling argument.
inline_numerical_claims.note: Order-of-magnitude estimate, not a pipeline-computed value

inline_numerical_claims.claim: kappa ~ 20-100 s^-1 for typical BEC parameters
inline_numerical_claims.support: Computed: Steinhauer 4.76, Heidelberg 101.9, Trento 21.4. Steinhauer is BELOW the stated range due to unusually small upstream velocity; paper text should say '~5-100 s^-1' to cover all three.

inline_numerical_claims.claim: a_s = 5.31 nm (100.4 a_0) per van Kempen 2002

inline_numerical_claims.support: Body text writes Gamma_H ~ gamma_1 (kappa^2 + c_s^2 k_H^2), then simplifies to gamma_1 kappa^2. The fixed implementation uses Gamma_H = (gamma_1+gamma_2)(kappa/c_s)^2 where gamma is [m^2/s]. These are dimensionally inequivalent unless one reinterprets gamma as a rate. The body derivation is schematic; the implementation matches the Lean theorem GammaH.
inline_numerical_claims.note: Body text gives a schematic identification; the pipeline identity is the Lean theorem GammaH with gamma_i in [m^2/s]

inline_numerical_claims.support: Matches Gamma_H/kappa = (gamma_1+gamma_2)(kappa/c_s)^2 / kappa up to the pi factor (which is the dimensionless mean-frequency integral). Paper drops gamma_2 and the equal-splitting constraint.
formulas.py beliaev_damping_rate returns np.sqrt(n_1D * a_s**3) * kappa**2 / c_s — matches

Dimensionally: (hbar/m)[m^2/s] x n_3D[m^-3] x sigma[m^2] = [m/s], not [s^-1]; or with n_1D[m^-1] x sigma[m^2] gives [m/s], still not a rate. The numerical range 10^-3 to 10^-1 also mixes conventions: the Lean/pipeline gamma_1 is [m^2/s] throughout. No pipeline code produces the quoted kinetic-regime delta_diss ~ 10^-4 to 10^-3.
inline_numerical_claims.note: Dimensional accounting of gamma_1 is inconsistent with the rest of the paper; the estimate reads like a placeholder

inline_numerical_claims.claim: Steinhauer step-profile kappa ~ 290 s^-1 gives T_H ~ 0.35 nK
inline_numerical_claims.support: T_H = hbar kappa / (2 pi k_B) = 1.0546e-34 * 290 / (2 pi * 1.381e-23) = 3.52e-10 K = 0.352 nK — matches within 1%

referenced_theorems.name: GammaH (def)
referenced_theorems.note: NEW in Phase 5u Wave 1 — horizon damping rate with k_H^2 factor

referenced_theorems.name: deltaDissFromTransport (def)
referenced_theorems.note: NEW — matches the Python compute_dissipative_correction identity

referenced_theorems.name: acousticMetric_det
referenced_theorems.note: Paper bullet 'det g_munu = -rho^2' — this theorem is the match

referenced_theorems.name: beliaev_damping_rate (Python)
referenced_theorems.note: Python-only; paper text cites it as UV-matching formula (not Lean-formal)

sorry_audit.note: All grep-hits for 'sorry' in Paper 1's modules are inside comments (section headers 'sorry Gaps', commented-out alternate proofs on lines SKDoubling:349, SKDoubling:634, SecondOrderSK:787, or descriptive docstring text). No active sorry tactics.

formal_verification_section_claims.claim: All 14 proof obligations machine-verified via Aristotle, zero remaining gaps
formal_verification_section_claims.verification: Consistent with sorry audit above; full Aristotle registry tracked elsewhere. No inconsistency surfaced.
Documented in SKDoubling.lean / FirstOrderKMS section; matches Aristotle finding.

layer_3_parameter_provenance.paper_dependencies_entry: PAPER_DEPENDENCIES['paper1_first_order'] lists 7 formulas, 3 platforms (Steinhauer, Heidelberg, Trento), 3 Lean modules

parameters.note: a_s = 5.31 nm (100.4 a_0) per van Kempen 2002 — paper matches

layer_3_parameter_provenance.summary: All 15 required parameters are LLM-verified (unblocks computation). None are human-verified (blocks paper submission per CHECK 15). Two Heidelberg/Trento trap frequencies are explicitly PROJECTED with an unsourced 500 Hz placeholder. No FAIL (no parameter is unverified).

layer_4_citation_integrity.note: Paper bibitems use 'Author:Year' convention; CITATION_REGISTRY uses 'AuthorYear' (no colon). Zero direct key matches, though author-level matches exist. This is either a paper-side or registry-side naming mismatch that should be reconciled before submission.

bibitems.note: No exact author-year entry in registry
Paper bibitem bundles 2016 + 2019 works; registry splits them (Steinhauer2016 and deNova2019)
Not in registry under any capitalization

action_items: Reconcile bibkey convention: rename paper \bibitem keys to AuthorYear, OR accept Author:Year as registry aliases.
Add missing registry entries for Jacobson1996, Son2002, Coutant2014, Zaremba1999 (all four are in paper 1's bibliography).
The Berti entry in the paper says 'Comptes Rendus Physique vol 25 (2025)'; prior reviews flagged this journal may be wrong — verify against arXiv:2408.17292 before submission.

layer_5_qualitative_claims.claim: dissipative corrections are suppressed at long wavelengths
layer_5_qualitative_claims.support: delta_diss ~ Gamma_H/kappa, and Gamma_H = (gamma_1+gamma_2)(kappa/c_s)^2 is bounded; for weak coupling (small gamma) it vanishes. Consistent with Lean gammaH_nonneg + deltaDissFromTransport_zero_iff.

layer_5_qualitative_claims.claim: Spin-sonic enhancement (c_density/c_spin)^2 ~ 100 could reach delta_diss ~ 10^-1 (kinetic) or 10^-3 (Beliaev)
layer_5_qualitative_claims.support: Order-of-magnitude argument; no pipeline code computes this with actual spin-channel transport coefficients. Numerical plausibility of the 100x factor depends on c_spin=0.1 mm/s, which is itself a toy value (paper notes 'if ... or lower').
layer_5_qualitative_claims.note: Speculative; relies on an assumed c_spin that is not in EXPERIMENTS['Trento']

layer_5_qualitative_claims.claim: Beliaev regime delta_diss ~ 10^-5 for typical BEC parameters
layer_5_qualitative_claims.support: Matches Steinhauer 2.38e-5 and Trento 1.41e-5; Heidelberg is 1.6e-3 (larger because of much larger kappa).

No pipeline computation; derived by plugging gamma_1^(kin) [quoted in s^-1] into a formula that expects gamma_1 [m^2/s]. Units do not balance. The qualitative direction (finite-T > T=0) is physically reasonable, but the quoted range is not derived.

layer_5_qualitative_claims.claim: 60x factor between tanh and step-profile kappa is geometric, not physical
layer_5_qualitative_claims.support: Correct scaling argument; a step potential has discontinuous derivative, so the effective kappa is set by the step height / width ratio, not by EFT. Not directly pipeline-testable but internally consistent.

layer_5_qualitative_claims.claim: delta_diss propto kappa, delta_disp propto kappa^2, so distinct scaling exponents identify the correction type
layer_5_qualitative_claims.support: From delta_diss = Gamma_H/kappa = (gamma_1+gamma_2) kappa/c_s^2 (linear in kappa, assuming gamma constant) and delta_disp ~ D^2 = (kappa xi/c_s)^2 (quadratic). Consistent.

layer_6_axiom_risk.note: Paper 1's Lean modules (AcousticMetric, SKDoubling, HawkingUniversality, SecondOrderSK, WKBAnalysis) are NOT listed as consumers of any entry in AXIOM_METADATA. The three registered axioms (non_abelian_center_discrete, gs_nogo_axiom, gapped_interface_axiom) belong to other papers (3, 7, 8). No paper-1 claim rests on a project axiom beyond the Lean/Mathlib standard.

explicit_hypotheses_from_registry.note: None of the 5 HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY entries (rokhlin_sigma_mod_16, modular_invariance_framing, c_minus_equals_8Nf, characteristic_square_mod_8, spin_bordism_iso_Z) list any Paper-1 theorem in their dependent_theorems fields. Paper 1 does not depend on these hypotheses.

fields.nature: Well-established BEC physics: c_s = sqrt(g n/m) > 0 whenever n > 0 and g > 0
fields.affected_claims: All Paper 1 hydrodynamic claims (acoustic metric, surface gravity, Hawking temperature)

fields.nature: Well-established BEC physics: n(x) > 0 throughout the condensate (no cavitation)
fields.affected_claims: All Paper 1 claims — required for Lorentzian signature and finite sound speed

fields.nature: Required by SK positivity (Im S_SK >= 0) — physical for T>=0 BEC with 3-phonon decay
fields.affected_claims: Paper 1, dissipative correction sign (delta_diss = Gamma_H/kappa >= 0 when gamma >= 0)

fields.nature: Same as gamma_1_nonneg, applied to anisotropic coefficient

fields.hypothesis: existence of x_H with v(x_H) = c_s(x_H)
fields.nature: Assumed to exist in any transonic background; the pipeline's solve_transonic_background constructs v(x) monotonically crossing c_s(x) so x_H is guaranteed for the tanh profile.
fields.affected_claims: All of Sec. IV (wave equation across horizon)

blocking_issues: Citation key convention mismatch — rename \bibitem keys from 'Author:Year' to 'AuthorYear' (or add aliases) so that CITATION_REGISTRY lookups succeed. Currently 0/11 bibitems resolve.
Four citations not in CITATION_REGISTRY at all: Jacobson1996, Son2002, Coutant2014, Zaremba1999 — add entries before submission.

Zaremba-regime dimensional inconsistency (eq. gamma_estimates_expanded, eq. delta_diss_zaremba) — gamma_1 units do not balance across body text vs. pipeline convention [m^2/s]. Either reformulate as Gamma_1 [s^-1] directly (with delta ~ Gamma_1/kappa) or rederive the numerical range.

Body-text relation Gamma_H ~ gamma_1 kappa^2 (eq. hawking_damping) drops the k_H^2 = (kappa/c_s)^2 factor that was just added to the implementation. Either add the (kappa/c_s)^2 explicitly or annotate it as schematic.

__main__ demo block in src/core/transonic_background.py (lines 406-441) still passes gamma_beliaev [s^-1] as gamma_1 [m^2/s] — produces nonsense delta_diss ~ 1e3 when run standalone. Not used for Table 1 (visualizations.py uses the correct beliaev_transport_coefficients extraction), but the demo should be fixed to avoid confusion.
non_blocking_warnings: All 15 platform parameters remain human_verified_date = None — blocks submission per CHECK 15, fine for draft.
kappa ~ 20-100 s^-1 sentence in Sec. II does not cover the 4.76 s^-1 Steinhauer value computed from the smooth profile. Suggest '~5-100 s^-1 depending on profile steepness'.
Spin-sonic c_spin ~ 0.1 mm/s is an assumed toy value, not a PARAMETER_PROVENANCE entry; the 100x enhancement factor is an order-of-magnitude projection.
summary: 24 PASS, 10 WARN, 0 FAIL on individual checks; overall issues_found due to (1) citation key mismatch blocking registry lookups, (2) Zaremba dimensional inconsistency, (3) human-verification pending on all platform parameters (blocks submission only). The core numerical result (Table 1) now reconciles with the pipeline after the k_H^2 bugfix. All Lean theorems referenced (new GammaH family and existing firstOrder_* / acousticMetric_*) are present with no active sorry.
